program_en: B.Sc. in Interdisciplinary Data Analytics & X Double Major Programme
program_chi: 跨學科數據分析及X理學士雙主修課程
learning_outcomes:
Interdisciplinary Data Analytics and X Double Major Programme_Learning
Outcomes 1. Major
Programme: Upon completion of
the Programme, students will have:
Knowledge outcomes Interdisciplinary knowledge By completing the programme,
graduates will have acquired knowledge of: (1) mathematics, statistics and business management; (2) computer-based information and data processing; (3) computational and statistical methods and tools. Critical
Thinking/Computational Thinking By completing the programme, graduates will have
developed the following abilities related to consciousness of data analytics: (1) consider problems from different perspectives; (2) identify and criticise the abuse and misuse of data analytics; (3) analyse quantitative evidence in a critical manner; (4) analyse and interpret large quantity of data. Skills
Outcomes Generic Competencies By completing the programme,
graduates will demonstrate competency in: (1) integrating knowledge in engineering, science and business, and
solving interdisciplinary problems; (2) communicating effectively in a scientific and business context; (3) working in teams; (4) programming and using information technology. Professional Skills By completing the programme,
graduates will have acquired professional skills on: (1) formulating data analytical and related engineering, science and
business problems; (2) analysing data accurately using mathematics, statistics, computer and
other technologies; (3) communicating
and conveying results of the analysis to both general public and professional
practitioners; (4) generating and evaluating alternatives, developing practical solutions
and proposing strategies in a global environment; (5) problem-solving in an inter-disciplinary context. Attitude/Value
Outcomes By completing the programme, graduates
will have acquired a positive attitude on: (1) awareness
of importance of data analytics and data ethics in daily life; (2) appreciation
of different aspects of knowledge in the era of Big Data; (3) commitment
to contribute to the development of GBA.

Major Programme
Requirement
Students
are required to complete a minimum of 90 units of courses. Students will
take the Interdisciplinary Data Analytics offered by CUHK as the 1st
major, and choose from one of the Majors offered by CUHK or
CUHK(SZ) as specified in 3. below as their 2nd Major. Some
courses offered by CUHK and CUHK(SZ) are equivalent/substitute. Please
refer to the list of
equivalent/substitute courses for details.

Explanatory
Notes:
1.  |  Students
enrolled in the Double Major Programme should normally spend four regular
terms at CUHK(SZ) during the four years of study, except otherwise
specified in 2. and 3. below. It applies to all students, including those
who select a 2nd Major offered by CUHK.
2.  |  Students
may apply for the Co-operative Education (Co-op) Programme (5 units) in
Year 2 or above. Students joining the Co-op Programme will undertake a
full-time placement in two specific work terms, including one summer
session and one regular term (i.e., term 1 or term 2). They have to spend
at least three regular terms at CUHK(SZ) during the four years of study.
3.  |  Students
who join term-time exchange programme have to spend at least three
regular terms at CUHK(SZ) during the four years of study.
4.  |  Students
enrolled in the Double Major programme can take equivalent/substitute
courses either at CUHK or CUHK(SZ). The list of equivalent/substitute
courses is available at this link (www.sci.cuhk.edu.hk/IDADM). Students should not take a course
and its equivalent/substitute course. Should they do so, only one course
will be counted towards the fulfilment of units requirements. Grades of
relevant equivalent/substitute courses taken at CUHK(SZ) will be transferred
to CUHK for the purpose of calculation of GPA, Major GPA and credit
units.
5.  |  Students
normally have to declare their 2nd Major by the end of their
first year of study.
6.  |  All
courses at 2000 and above level and courses labeled as # in the
requirements of the 1st Major Programme, as well as 3 units of
research component course offered by the 2nd Major, will be
included in the calculation of Major GPA for honours classification.
7.  |  Courses
taken to fulfil the requirements of the 1st Major cannot be
counted towards the fulfilment of requirements of the 2nd Major, and vice versa.
8.  |  Under
very special circumstances and subject to approval by the Major
programmes concerned, students may opt out of the Double Major Programme
and register for the 1st Major programme only, and they will
be required to take an additional 3 units of research component course
offered by the 2nd Major to fulfill the unit requirement of
the 1st Major which is 60 units. The 3 units of research
component will also be included in the calculation of Major GPA. The
decision to opt out is irrevocable. Students
who have been approved to opt out from the Double Major Programme should
still observe the requirements in 1. above. If the students have not yet
declared a 2nd Major at the time of opting out, they should
fulfil the requirements of one of the areas under the 1st Major and take the additional 3 units of research component offered by
the 2nd Major corresponding to the particular area.
[a]  |  Non-JUPAS
and JUPAS admittees without HKDSE Mathematics Extended Modules I or II
may consider taking STAT1011 in advance for enriching statistics
knowledge, if necessary.
[b]  |  For
students who choose Integrated BBA programme as 2nd Major,
they are required to take 15 units from Group A. Students will graduate
with General Business Concentration and are not allowed to declare Minor
in Business.

area code “EIE” changed to “ECE” with effect from 2025-26.
